mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future equity cash flows, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.3pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to generate positive 60-day forward returns in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in the index of industrial production below its 6-month moving average signals immediate supply-side contraction that triggers short-term equity sell-offs over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 6-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: When core inflation remains above 3.0% while the federal funds rate stays below 4.5%, markets price in aggressive future tightening, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 60% of periods meeting the inflation-rate threshold condition.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate profit margins, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show negative correlation with PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP coinciding with falling retail sales confirms a demand contraction that reduces earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 20d returns in > 65% of quarters where both GDPC1 and RSAFS decline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production and housing starts reflect expanding economic capacity and construction activity, which supports near-term equity valuations and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where both INDPRO and HOUST increase month-over-month.
